messages.content: In an email, I asked an acquaintance that I am not close with how they are doing. They said they recently had a surgery and all went well. What would be a kind way to reply to that before returning to the rest of my reply?
I'm glad to hear that your surgery went well!
i feel like it needs another sentence
You must have been a little nervous, but I'm happy to know that things worked out positively.
something other than that
That's great news! I hope your recovery is going well so far.
What else?
I'm really pleased to hear that you came through the surgery okay. Please take good care of yourself as you recuperate.
What else?
It's good to know that everything went according to plan during your surgery. Please don't hesitate to let me know if there's anything I can do to assist you during your recovery.
That's too much for an aquaintance

You're right, for an acquaintance, it might be better to keep the response short and sweet. In that case, you could simply say: "I'm glad to hear that your surgery went well. Wishing you a speedy recovery!"
what if I don't know if they've recovered yet?
In that case, you could say: "I'm glad to hear that your surgery went well. Hope you're feeling better soon!"
what else?
You could say: "It's comforting to know that your surgery went well. Let me know if there's anything I can do to help you during your recovery."
Again, too much
I see. In that case, a shorter reply could be: "Glad to hear that your surgery was successful. Wishing you a quick recovery!"
Not too much text, just that it is too much to offer to help them when I don't know them as well.
I understand. In that case, you could say: "Thank you for letting me know. I'm glad to hear that your surgery went well. Take care and best wishes for your recovery!"
what else
Another option could be: "I'm relieved to hear that your surgery went well. Wishing you all the best as you recover."
This is an older person, and I'm a younger person. It wouldn't be appropriate for me to talk about myself here to relate.
I see. In that case, you could say something like this: "I'm glad to hear that your surgery went well. It's great news, and I hope your recovery continues to go smoothly."
No no, I mean, i was asking you

My apologies for misunderstanding your previous message. As an AI language model, I don't have personal experiences I can share, but I can help you find a way to express empathy and support. A suitable response could be "I'm grateful to hear that your surgery went well. Please take care of yourself and let me know if there's anything I can do to assist you during your recovery."
